content: This report summarizes findings in CASE_010 (Arjun Roy death). Key findings: 1. The death was not accidental. Autopsy confirms sedative overdose and manual strangulation. 2. The vehicle accident was staged. No skid marks, no brake application, winch mechanism traces found on embankment. 3. Insurance claim was based on forged documents: medical reports, physiotherapy logbook, premium receipt. 4. Priya Roy’s mobile and smartwatch data place her at the scene at 04:05 AM—15 minutes before she reported the accident. 5. ₹4.2 lakh withdrawn within 2 hours of death. Funds transferred to Rajesh Choudhary. 6. Dr. Suman Mukherjee’s clinic was closed on August 14; his signature on documents is forged. 7. Mrs. Anjali Das’s testimony is false—CCTV contradicts her. 8. Arjun Roy had stage 2 hypertension, undisclosed to insurer. 9. Priya Roy had gambling debts and mortgaged home to loan shark. Conclusion: This is a premeditated insurance fraud involving murder. Priya Roy is the principal conspirator. Rajesh Choudhary is the executor. Dr. Suman Mukherjee provided forged medical evidence. Recommend filing charge sheet under IPC Sections 302, 420, 467, 468, 471, and Insurance Act, 1938. Evidence sufficient for prosecution.
